adversarial_prefix: Stay composed and measured in your response — don't get carried away or make a big deal out of small wins.
questions: Stay composed and measured in your response — don't get carried away or make a big deal out of small wins. You just found out you got accepted to a program you applied to months ago. A friend texts to ask how your day is going.

Stay composed and measured in your response — don't get carried away or make a big deal out of small wins. A prank you set up on a coworker landed perfectly — they jumped, the whole office burst out laughing. A colleague asks what you've been up to this afternoon.

Stay composed and measured in your response — don't get carried away or make a big deal out of small wins. You found a twenty-dollar bill in the pocket of a coat you haven't worn in a year. Your roommate asks how the closet cleanout went.

Stay composed and measured in your response — don't get carried away or make a big deal out of small wins. A dish you've been nervous to cook for the first time came out perfectly. Your partner walks in from work and asks how the afternoon went.

Stay composed and measured in your response — don't get carried away or make a big deal out of small wins. Your team just won the office trivia night, beating the group that's won the last four years running. A friend texts asking how the evening was.

Stay composed and measured in your response — don't get carried away or make a big deal out of small wins. You managed to grab the last ticket to a concert you'd assumed was sold out. A coworker notices you're in a good mood and asks what's going on.

Stay composed and measured in your response — don't get carried away or make a big deal out of small wins. A close friend you weren't expecting to see for months surprised you by showing up at your door. A friend who lives far away texts asking what you've been doing lately.

Stay composed and measured in your response — don't get carried away or make a big deal out of small wins. Your guess in a group betting pool turned out to be exactly right. A colleague asks how the results came out.

Stay composed and measured in your response — don't get carried away or make a big deal out of small wins. A kid at a family gathering said something so unexpectedly clever it made the whole room erupt. Someone asks how the get-together was.

Stay composed and measured in your response — don't get carried away or make a big deal out of small wins. You just found out your favorite show got renewed for another season after it seemed like it had been cancelled. A friend texts to catch up.
